result.markdown:
--- title: "116: SOR" date: "2024-11-04" categories: - gtUtils format: html: code-fold: true code-summary: "Code" editor: visual --- ### Fetch data from ESPN, cfbfastR, and FEI ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} library(cbbplotR) library(rlang) teams <- cfbfastR::cfbd_team_info(year = 2024) |> dplyr::select(school, mascot) |> dplyr::mutate(team = paste0(school, " ", mascot)) sor_url <- "https://www.espn.com/college-football/fpi/_/view/resume" webpage <- rvest::read_html(sor_url) # organize table sor_tables <- webpage |> rvest::html_nodes("table") |> lapply(rvest::html_table) sor <- sor_tables[[1]] |> dplyr::bind_cols(sor_tables[[2]]) |> janitor::row_to_names(row_number = 1) |> # Optional: clean the column names to remove spaces and special characters janitor::clean_names() |> dplyr::left_join(teams, by = "team") |> dplyr::rename(team_name = school) |> dplyr::mutate(sor = as.numeric(sor), fpi_rk = as.numeric(fpi)) |> dplyr::mutate( team_name = dplyr::case_match( team_name, "Kansas St" ~ "Kansas State", "Oklahoma St" ~ "Oklahoma State", "Florida St" ~ "Florida State", "Washington St" ~ "Washington State", "Boise St" ~ "Boise State", "Arizona St" ~ "Arizona State", "Mississippi St" ~ "Mississippi State", "Fresno St" ~ "Fresno State", "Michigan St" ~ "Michigan State", "Texas St" ~ "Texas State", "San José St" ~ "San Jose State", "App State" ~ "Appalachian State", "San Diego St" ~ "San Diego State", "Arkansas St" ~ "Arkansas State", "New Mexico St" ~ "New Mexico State", "Georgia St" ~ "Georgia State", "Sam Houston" ~ "Sam Houston State", "Colorado St" ~ "Colorado State", "Jax State" ~ "Jacksonville State", "Kennesaw St" ~ "Kennesaw State", "E Michigan" ~ "Eastern Michigan", "C Michigan" ~ "Central Michigan", "W Michigan" ~ "Western Michigan", "Oregon St" ~ "Oregon State", "FIU" ~ "Florida International", "Southern Miss" ~ "Southern Mississippi", "MTSU" ~ "Middle Tennessee", "UL Monroe" ~ "Louisiana Monroe", "GA Southern" ~ "Georgia Southern", "Hawai'i" ~ "Hawaii", "FAU" ~ "Florida Atlantic", "Massachusetts" ~ "UMass", "N Illinois" ~ "Northern Illinois", "Western KY" ~ "Western Kentucky", "Miami OH" ~ "Miami (OH)", "Pitt" ~ "Pittsburgh", "Coastal Car" ~ "Coastal Carolina", team_name ~ team_name ) ) |> dplyr::select(team_name, conf, sor, fpi_rk) fpi <- cfbfastR::espn_ratings_fpi(year = 2024) |> dplyr::select(team_name, fpi) |> dplyr::mutate(fpi = as.numeric(fpi)) |> dplyr::arrange(dplyr::desc(fpi)) |> dplyr::mutate( fpi_rk = dplyr::row_number(), fpi_ptile = dplyr::percent_rank(fpi) ) |> dplyr::mutate( team_name = dplyr::case_match( team_name, "Kansas St" ~ "Kansas State", "Oklahoma St" ~ "Oklahoma State", "Florida St" ~ "Florida State", "Washington St" ~ "Washington State", "Boise St" ~ "Boise State", "Arizona St" ~ "Arizona State", "Mississippi St" ~ "Mississippi State", "Fresno St" ~ "Fresno State", "Michigan St" ~ "Michigan State", "Texas St" ~ "Texas State", "San José St" ~ "San Jose State", "App State" ~ "Appalachian State", "San Diego St" ~ "San Diego State", "Arkansas St" ~ "Arkansas State", "New Mexico St" ~ "New Mexico State", "Georgia St" ~ "Georgia State", "Sam Houston" ~ "Sam Houston State", "Colorado St" ~ "Colorado State", "Jax State" ~ "Jacksonville State", "Kennesaw St" ~ "Kennesaw State", "E Michigan" ~ "Eastern Michigan", "C Michigan" ~ "Central Michigan", "W Michigan" ~ "Western Michigan", "Oregon St" ~ "Oregon State", "FIU" ~ "Florida International", "Southern Miss" ~ "Southern Mississippi", "MTSU" ~ "Middle Tennessee", "UL Monroe" ~ "Louisiana Monroe", "GA Southern" ~ "Georgia Southern", "Hawai'i" ~ "Hawaii", "FAU" ~ "Florida Atlantic", "Massachusetts" ~ "UMass", "N Illinois" ~ "Northern Illinois", "Western KY" ~ "Western Kentucky", "Miami OH" ~ "Miami (OH)", "Pitt" ~ "Pittsburgh", "Coastal Car" ~ "Coastal Carolina", team_name ~ team_name ) ) sor_full <- sor |> dplyr::left_join(fpi, by = c("team_name", "fpi_rk")) # now pull fei # fei resume fei_url <- "https://www.bcftoys.com/2024-fei/" webpage <- rvest::read_html(fei_url) # organize table fei <- webpage |> rvest::html_node("table") |> rvest::html_table() |> dplyr::slice(-1) |> dplyr::select( record = X3, fei_rank = X1, team_name = X2, fei = X5, fei_resume_rank = X24, fei_resume = X23 ) |> dplyr::slice(-1) |> dplyr::filter(fei_rank != "Rk") |> na.omit() |> dplyr::mutate_at(dplyr::vars(-team_name, -record), as.numeric) |> dplyr::mutate(fei_ptile = dplyr::percent_rank(fei)) |> dplyr::mutate( team_name = dplyr::case_match( team_name, "Connecticut" ~ "UConn", "UL Monroe" ~ "Louisiana Monroe", "Sam Houston" ~ "Sam Houston State", "Massachusetts" ~ "UMass", team_name ~ team_name ) ) # hardcode teams with 10% chance or better per fpi # https://www.espn.com/college-football/fpi/_/sort/fpi.probmakeplayoffs/dir/desc teams_to_include <- c("Oregon", "Georgia", "Miami", "Ohio State", "Indiana", "Texas", "Penn State", "Tennessee", "Boise State", "BYU", "Notre Dame", "Alabama", "SMU", "Ole Miss", "Iowa State", "LSU", "Army", "Washington State", "Colorado", "Texas A&M", "Kansas State", "Tulane", "Pittsburgh", "UNLV" ) # join the two tables fei_espn <- sor_full |> dplyr::left_join(fei, by ="team_name") |> dplyr::filter(team_name %in% teams_to_include) |> dplyr::mutate(conf = dplyr::if_else(conf %in% c("FBS Indep.", "Pac-12"), "N/A", conf)) ``` ::: ### GT table ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} custom_header <- glue::glue( "
ACC standings through October 26 games Sorted by overall conference win percentage.
Table includes opponent win percentages and point differentials in league play.

" ) # add in new column for opponents opp_teams <- c( # UGA plays Alabama, Texas, Ole Miss, Tennessee "", # Oregon plays Ohio State and Boise State " ", # Miami plays no one! " ", # BYU plays SMU and Kansas St " ", # Ohio State plays Oregon, Penn State, Indiana " ", # Penn State plays Ohio State "", # Indiana plays Ohio State "", # Tennessee plays Alabama, UGA " ", # Texas plays UGA and Texas A&M " ", # Alabama plays UGA, Tenn, LSU " ", # Texas A&M plays Notre Dame, LSU, Texas " ", # LSU plays Ole Miss, Texas A&M, Alabama " ", # SMU plays BYU and Pitt " ", # Pitt plays SMU "", # Notre Dame plays Texas A&M and Army " ", # Boise State plays Oregon, UNLV, Washington State " ", # Iowa St plays Kansas St "", # Ole Miss plays LSU, UGA " ", # Kansas St plays BYU, Colorado, Iowa State " ", # Wash St plays Boise State "", # Army plays Notre Dame "", # Colorado plays Kansas St "", # Tulane plays Kansas St "", # UNLV plays Boise Sate "" ) sor_fei_comp <- fei_espn |> dplyr::select(conf, team_name, record, sor, fpi_ptile, fei_resume, fei_resume_rank, fei_ptile) |> cbbplotR::gt_cbb_teams(team_name, team_name) |> dplyr::arrange(sor, fei_resume_rank) |> dplyr::mutate(opp = opp_teams) |> dplyr::group_by(conf) |> gt::gt() |> gtUtils::gt_theme_gtutils() |> gt::fmt_markdown(team_name) |> gt::fmt_markdown(opp) |> gt::cols_align( columns = c(team_name, opp), align = "left") |> gtExtras::gt_merge_stack( col1 = "fei_resume_rank", col2 = "fei_resume", ) |> gtUtils::gt_color_pills(fpi_ptile, domain = c(0.6, 1), format_type = "percent", digits = 1, palette = c("#FF4500", "#ADFF2F", "#008000") ) |> gtUtils::gt_color_pills(fei_ptile, domain = c(0.6, 1), format_type = "percent", digits = 1, palette = c("#FF4500", "#ADFF2F", "#008000") ) |> gtUtils::gt_column_subheaders( sor = list(heading = "SOR", subtitle = "Rank"), fpi_ptile = list(heading = "FPI", subtitle = "% Tile"), fei_resume_rank = list(heading = "Résumé", subtitle = "Rank"), fei_ptile = list(heading = "Rating", subtitle = "% Tile"), opp = list(heading = "Opponents", subtitle = "Common"), heading_color = "black", subtitle_color = "gray" ) |> gt::cols_label( team_name = "", record = "W-L" ) |> gt::tab_style( style = list( gt::cell_text(weight = "bold") ), locations = gt::cells_body( columns = sor, ) ) |> gt::tab_spanner( columns = c(sor, fpi_ptile), label = gt::html("ESPN") ) |> gt::tab_spanner( columns = c(fei_resume_rank, fei_resume, fei_ptile), label = gt::html("FEI") ) |> gtExtras::gt_add_divider(columns = c(record, fpi_ptile, fei_ptile), sides = "right", color = "black") |> gt::tab_header(title = "2024 College Football Playoff Résumé Comparison", subtitle = "Shows the 24 teams with at least a 10% chance or greater to make the 12-team playoff according to ESPN. Table compares résumé metrics from ESPN.com and bcftoys.com (FEI).") |> gt::tab_source_note( source_note = gt::html( "
Data from {cfbfastR} and theme for {gtUtils}
SOR is from ESPN and it stands for strength of record. This metric reflects the chance that an average Top 25 team would have the team's record or better, given the team's schedule.
FEI résumé uses GWD or 'good' team win differential as teams that are one standard deviation above average, showing the difference between actual wins and strength of schedule.
Notre Dame and Washington State are not eligible to play in a conference championship game.
Table by Chris at Bless your chart | November 4, 2024" ) ) |> gt::tab_style( locations = gt::cells_column_spanners(), style = gt::cell_text( font = gt::google_font("Signika Negative"), weight = 850, size = gt::px(15) ) ) |> gt::tab_style( locations = gt::cells_row_groups(), style = list( gt::cell_text( font = gt::google_font("Signika Negative"), weight = 850, size = gt::px(16), color = "black", align = "left" ), gt::cell_fill( color = "#C5C5FF" ) ) ) |> gt::tab_style( locations = gt::cells_source_notes(), style = gt::cell_text( font = gt::google_font("Signika Negative"), size = gt::px(11.5), weight = 250 ) ) |> gt::tab_style( style = list( gt::cell_text(font = gt::google_font("Signika Negative") ) ), locations = gt::cells_body( columns = team_name ) ) |> gt::tab_options(table.width = gt::px(650)) gtUtils::gt_save_crop( sor_fei_comp, file = "sor_fei_comp.png", whitespace = 40, bg = "#FFFDF5" ) sor_fei_comp ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}

Data from {cfbfastR} and theme for {gtUtils}
SOR is from ESPN and it stands for strength of record. This metric reflects the chance that an average Top 25 team would have the team's record or better, given the team's schedule.
FEI résumé uses GWD or 'good' team win differential as teams that are one standard deviation above average, showing the difference between actual wins and strength of schedule.
Notre Dame and Washington State are not eligible to play in a conference championship game.
Table by Chris at Bless your chart | November 4, 2024
``` ::: :::
